L1.area: Peer-to-peer network discovery and topic registration protocol handling

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where an unsigned integer index received from a remote peer is compared against zero without first converting it to a signed type. Because the comparison on the unsigned value is always false, a crafted large index wraps to a negative value when subsequently cast to a signed integer for array access, bypassing bounds validation and causing an out-of-bounds panic that crashes the node.

L3.area: P2P discovery protocol topic registration and validation logic
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the topic registration validation of the peer-to-peer discovery protocol. An unsigned integer index received from a remote peer is compared against zero without first converting it to a signed type. Because the comparison on the unsigned value is always false, a crafted large index wraps to a negative value when subsequently cast to a signed integer for array access, bypassing bounds validation and causing a denial-of-service crash.

ground_truth.reason: The checkTopicRegister function compares an unsigned integer index against zero without casting to a signed type first, so the negative check is always false. A remote attacker can send a large index that wraps negative on conversion, bypassing bounds validation and crashing the node.
